seo.primaryKeyword: how many questions can you miss on AP Psych
seo.title: How Many Questions Can You Miss on AP Psych and Still Get a 5?
seo.description: Find out how many AP Psych multiple-choice questions you may be able to miss and still earn a 3, 4, or 5 using estimated score ranges.
seo.h1: How Many Questions Can You Miss on AP Psych?

content.directAnswer: How many questions you can miss on AP Psych depends on your target score and FRQ performance, not just the multiple-choice section by itself. Missing 10 questions can still leave strong score options, while missing 25 usually creates much more pressure on the FRQs and makes a top score less comfortable.

sections.heading: Missed Questions Are Only Part of the Score
sections.body:
A missed-question estimate is useful, but it only covers the multiple-choice section. Your final AP Psych estimate also depends on the points you earn across the two FRQs, which is why students with the same number of missed questions can still end up with different score estimates.

This is the main reason there is no universal answer to how many questions can you miss on AP Psych. The right answer changes depending on whether you are aiming for a 3, 4, or 5 and whether your free-response section felt strong or shaky.

sections.heading: Common Missed-Question Scenarios
sections.body:
Try scenarios such as missing 10, 15, 20, or 25 MCQ questions and compare the results with different FRQ scores. For example, 60 correct out of 75 is a much stronger starting point than 50 correct, but FRQ points still matter.

The point of these scenarios is not to memorize one exact threshold. It is to see how much room you have left after the multiple-choice section and whether the FRQs need to save the estimate.

sections.heading: Convert Missed Questions to MCQ Correct
sections.body:
The calculator asks for MCQ correct, so subtract missed questions from 75. Missing 10 means entering 65 correct, missing 15 means 60 correct, missing 20 means 55 correct, and missing 25 means 50 correct.

That conversion matters because students often think in terms of what they got wrong after the exam, while the tool estimates scores from what you got right.

sections.heading: Missing Questions for a 3, 4, or 5
sections.body:
A score goal of 3 gives you more room for missed MCQ questions than a goal of 5. If you are aiming for a 5, test conservative FRQ assumptions because the top score leaves less margin for error.

If you are aiming for a 3 or 4, focus on whether your total composite estimate clears the relevant band rather than obsessing over one exact missed-question number. The same MCQ total can look fine for a 3 but risky for a 5.

sections.heading: How to Estimate After the Exam
sections.body:
After the exam, start with your best memory of how many MCQ questions you got right. Then enter low, medium, and high FRQ estimates to see the range of possible AP scores.

This is much more useful than picking one guess and treating it as final. If your score range changes a lot between conservative and optimistic FRQs, your result is probably close to a boundary.

faqs.question: Is this calculator official?
faqs.answer: No. This calculator is an independent estimate and is not affiliated with or endorsed by College Board.

faqs.question: Can actual AP score cutoffs vary?
faqs.answer: Yes. Actual score thresholds can vary by year and exam form, so missed-question estimates are only planning ranges.

faqs.question: How many AP Psych MCQ questions are there?
faqs.answer: This calculator uses 75 multiple-choice questions for AP Psych planning scenarios.

faqs.question: Can a strong FRQ score offset missed MCQ questions?
faqs.answer: Yes. A strong FRQ score can improve your composite estimate, but it does not erase a very low MCQ total.

faqs.question: Can I miss 20 questions and still get a 5?
faqs.answer: It may be possible in some estimate scenarios if your FRQ performance is strong, but it is not a safe assumption. Test the exact MCQ and FRQ combination in the calculator.

faqs.question: Should I estimate missed or correct questions?
faqs.answer: The calculator uses MCQ correct. If you know how many you missed, subtract that number from 75 and enter the result.

faqs.question: Is missing 15 questions usually better than missing 25?
faqs.answer: Yes. Missing 15 leaves 60 MCQ correct, while missing 25 leaves 50 MCQ correct. That difference can matter a lot near score cutoffs.

faqs.question: How many questions can you miss on AP Psych and still get a 3?
faqs.answer: Usually more than you could miss for a 5, because a passing estimate is less demanding. The exact answer still depends on your FRQ scores, so it is better to test combinations than rely on one blanket number.

faqs.question: How many questions can you miss on AP Psych and still get a 4?
faqs.answer: A 4 generally leaves less room than a 3 but more than a 5. If your FRQs are strong, you can sometimes absorb more missed MCQ questions than you expect.
